displayTitle: A Field That Never Holds Still
kicker: ET LEVEL I // GENERATING EDDY CURRENTS

beats.narration: Eddy current testing starts with a coil and alternating current. Drive that coil, and you build a magnetic field that never holds still.

payload.title: GENERATING EDDY CURRENTS
payload.sub: An AC coil makes a field that never stops moving.

beats.narration: Direct current builds a strong but static field that induces nothing. Alternating current expands, collapses, and reverses every cycle, so the flux is always changing.
Bring a conductive part into that field and induction is automatic. The induced voltage drives electrons around closed loops. Those are eddy currents.

payload.heading: From AC coil to eddy currents
payload.note: AC COIL → CHANGING FLUX → INDUCED EMF → EDDY CURRENTS

beats.narration: The part acts as a transformer's shorted secondary. Faraday sets the current's size; Lenz sets its direction, opposite the coil, coupled across an air gap.

payload.heading: Coil and part as a transformer

beats.narration: Three conditions, no exceptions: a changing field, a conductive part, and coupling between them. Remove any one and the eddy currents vanish.

payload.sub: Remove any one and the eddy currents vanish.
